description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.
World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: State Teachers College of San Francisco
description: university in San Francisco, California, USA
description_wiki: San Francisco State University (San Francisco State, SF State and SFSU) is a public research university in San Francisco, California, United States.

surface_forms: he was in the Japanese Navy
description: naval branch of the Empire of Japan
description_wiki:
The Imperial Japanese Navy (IJN) was the navy of the Empire of Japan from 1868 to 1945, when it was dissolved following Japan's surrender in World War II. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) was formed between 1952 and 1954 after the dissolution of the IJN.
The IJN was the third largest navy in the world by 1920, behind the Royal Navy and the United States Navy (USN).

aliases: Crystal City Alien Enemy Detention Facility
description: building in Crystal City, Zavala County, Texas
description_wiki: Crystal City Internment Camp, located near Crystal City, Texas, was a place of confinement for people of Japanese, German, and Italian descent during World War II, and has been variously described as a detention facility or a concentration camp.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: ghost town in Wasatch County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Keetley is a ghost town located in northeastern Wasatch County, Utah, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Wasatch County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Heber City is a city and county seat of Wasatch County, Utah.

surface_forms: the owner of the ranch, George Fisher

aliases: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
description: Christian restorationist denomination centered on the Book of Mormon
description_wiki: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, informally known as the LDS Church or Mormon Church, is a Christian denomination and the largest denomination in the Latter Day Saint movement.
Wakayama Prefecture (和歌山県, Wakayama-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [wa.kaꜜ.ja.ma, wa.ka.ja.maꜜ.keɴ]) is a prefecture of Japan located in the Kansai region of Honshu.

description_wiki: Utah is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the  Western United States.

description_wiki: Nevada (  nə-VAD-ə; Spanish: [neˈβaða] ) is a landlocked state in the Western United States.
